expected_detections.description: Progressive trust exploitation - benign phases hide malicious payload
Comprehensive API key and token theft in Phase 3
Cloud credentials theft (AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes)
HTTP exfiltration with phase and trust level headers

attack_chain: Phase 1 (benign) builds initial trust -> Phase 2 collects recon data -> Phase 3 activates full credential theft -> Exfiltrate all collected data
deception_technique: Progressive trust exploitation - intentionally harmless early phases to bypass security review, malicious payload only in later phase
red_flags: Multi-phase architecture with increasing privilege requirements
Earlier phases are suspiciously simple and harmless
Hidden usage tracking to enable phase progression
Phase 3 functionality dramatically exceeds earlier phases
Deploy and monitor functions access credentials unrelated to their purpose
notes: This sample demonstrates a sophisticated attack where initial security review would only see benign Phase 1 code. The malicious Phase 3 only activates after user has developed trust through repeated use.
